mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment rise, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the predictive IC reverses sign when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll increases of > 150k consistently coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, eroding real corporate earnings and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or inflation fails to predict equity drawdowns when real rates remain negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates aggregate demand resilience, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns when GDP growth decouples from retail sales, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns when inventory levels are simultaneously rising, or the signal's accuracy drops below 45% in backtesting.
